volumeTitle: Persians. Seven against Thebes. Suppliants. Prometheus Bound
label: Prometheus Bound (Section 987 - Section 1090)

sections.label: Section 987
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ σὺ παῖς τε κἄτι τοῦδ ἀνούστερος ,
sections.translation: And are you not a child and even more witless than a child if you expect to learn anything from me ? There is no torment or device by which

sections.label: Section 990
sections.translation: Zeus shall induce me to utter this until these injurious fetters are loosed . So then , let his blazing lightning be hurled , and with the white wings of the snow and thunders of earthquake let him confound the reeling world .

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: γνάμψει γὰρ οὐδὲν τῶνδέ μ ὥστε καὶ φράσαι [*]
sections.translation: For nothing of this shall bend my will even to tell at whose hands he is fated to be hurled from his sovereignty .

sections.label: Section 997
sections.original: ὅρα νυν εἴ σοι ταῦτ ἀρωγὰ φαίνεται .
sections.translation: Look now whether this course seems to profit you .

sections.label: Section 998
sections.original: ὦπται [*] πάλαι δὴ καὶ βεβούλευται τάδε .
sections.translation: Long ago has this my course been foreseen and resolved .

sections.label: Section 999
sections.translation: Bend your will , perverse fool , oh bend your will at last

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.original: πρὸς τὰς παρούσας πημονὰς ὀρθῶς φρονεῖν .
sections.translation: to wisdom in face of your present sufferings .

sections.label: Section 1001
sections.original: ὀχλεῖς μάτην με κῦμ ὅπως παρηγορῶν .
sections.translation: In vain you trouble me , as though it were a wave you try to persuade . Never think that , through terror at the will of Zeus , I shall become womanish and , with hands upturned , aping woman ’ s ways ,

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.translation: shall importune my greatly hated enemy to release me from these bonds . I am far , far from that .

sections.label: Section 1007
sections.original: λέγων ἔοικα πολλὰ καὶ μάτην ἐρεῖν :
sections.translation: I think that by speaking much I will only speak in vain ; for you are not soothed nor are you softened by my entreaties . You take the bit in your teeth like a new-harnessed

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: πῶλος βιάζει καὶ πρὸς ἡνίας μάχει .
sections.translation: colt and struggle against the reins . Yet it is a paltry device that prompts your vehemence , for in the foolish-minded mere self-will of itself avails less than anything at all . But if you will not be won to belief by my words ,

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: οἷός σε χειμὼν καὶ κακῶν τρικυμία
sections.translation: think of what a tempest and a towering wave of woe shall break upon you past escape . First , the Father will shatter this jagged cliff with thunder and lightning-flame , and will entomb your frame , while the rock shall still hold you clasped in its embrace .

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.translation: But when you have completed a long stretch of time , you shall come back again to the light . Then indeed the winged hound of Zeus , the ravening eagle , coming an unbidden banqueter the whole day long , with savage appetite shall tear your body piecemeal into great rents and feast his fill

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.translation:
upon your liver until it is black with gnawing .
Look for no term of this your agony until some god shall appear to take upon himself your woes and of his own free will descend into the sunless realm of Death and the dark deeps of Tartarus .

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: πρὸς ταῦτα βούλευ : ὡς ὅδ οὐ πεπλασμένος
sections.translation: Therefore be advised , since this is no counterfeited vaunting but utter truth ; for the mouth of Zeus does not know how to utter falsehood , but will bring to pass every word . May you consider warily and reflect , and never deem

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.translation: stubbornness better than wise counsel .

sections.label: Section 1036
sections.original: ἡμῖν μὲν Ἑρμῆς οὐκ ἄκαιρα φαίνεται
sections.translation: To us , at least , Hermes seems not to speak untimely ; for he bids you to lay aside your stubbornness and seek the good counsel of wisdom . Be advised ! It is shameful for the wise to persist in error .

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.translation: No news to me , in truth , is the message this fellow has proclaimed so noisily . Yet for enemy to suffer ill from enemy is no disgrace . Therefore let the lightning ’ s forked curl be cast upon my head and let the sky

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.translation: be convulsed with thunder and the wrack of savage winds ; let the hurricane shake the earth from its rooted base , and let the waves of the sea mingle with their savage surge the courses

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.translation: of the stars in heaven ; and let him lift me on high and hurl me down to black Tartarus with the swirling floods of stern Necessity : do what he will , me he shall never bring to death .

sections.label: Section 1054
sections.translation: Such indeed are the thoughts and the words

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.translation: one hears from men deranged . Where does his prayer fall short of raving ? Where does he abate his frenzy ?— But , at all events , may you who sympathize with his anguish ,

sections.label: Section 1060
sections.original: μετά ποι [*] χωρεῖτ ἐκ τῶνδε θοῶς ,
sections.translation: withdraw in haste from this spot so that the relentless roar of the thunder does not stun your senses .

sections.label: Section 1063
sections.original: ἄλλο τι φώνει καὶ παραμυθοῦ μ
sections.translation: Use some other strain and urge me to some other course in which you are likely to convince me . This utterance

sections.label: Section 1065
sections.translation: in your flood of speech is , I think , past all endurance . How do you charge me to practise baseness ? With him I am content to suffer any fate ; for I have learned to detest traitors , and there is no pest

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.translation: I abhor more than this .

sections.label: Section 1071
sections.translation: Well then , bear my warning in memory and do not blame your fortune when you are caught in the toils of calamity ; nor ever say that it was Zeus who cast you

sections.label: Section 1075
sections.original: πῆμ εἰσέβαλεν : μὴ δῆτ , αὐταὶ δ
sections.translation: into suffering unforeseen . Not so , but blame yourselves . For well forewarned , and not suddenly or secretly shall you be entangled in the inextricable net of calamity by reason of your folly . Exit

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.translation: Indeed , now it has passed from word to deed — the earth rocks , the echoing thunder-peal from the depths rolls roaring past me ; the fiery wreathed lightning-flashes flare forth , and whirlwinds toss the

sections.label: Section 1085
sections.translation: swirling dust ; the blasts of all the winds leap forth and set in hostile array their embattled strife ; the sky is confounded with the deep . Behold , this stormy turmoil advances against me visibly ,

sections.label: Section 1090
sections.translation: sent by Zeus to frighten me . O holy mother mine , O you firmament that revolves the common light of all , you see the wrongs I suffer ! Amid thunder and lightning Prometheus vanishes from sight ; and with him disappear the daughters of Oceanus
